data.title: Mechanism Capital founder doubles Bitcoin position with a $200M long
data.description:
Andrew Kang, founder of the crypto venture firm Mechanism Capital, has seemingly doubled down on his bet that Bitcoin will gain in price with a $200 million long position, onchain data shows.  “Andrew Kang just doubled his Bitcoin position,” crypto analytics firm Arkham said in an April 12 X post. It explained a crypto address tied to Kang made another $100 million long bet on Bitcoin (BTC) with an expected profit, or loss, of $6. 8 million
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/mechanism-capital-founder-doubles-bitcoin-position-200-million-long?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Japan's 20-Year Bond Yield Reaches Highest Level Since 2004
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the yield on Japan's 20-year government bonds has risen to 2.435%, marking the highest level since 2004. This increase reflects ongoing shifts in the financial markets and investor sentiment.

data.title: SyncOrbit to launch universal account function portal on MetaMask
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SyncOrbit’s announcement, MetaMask will soon integrate SyncOrbit’s Universal Account function entrance into the application, allowing users to create and manage SyncOrbit universal accounts directly through the familiar wallet interface, and achieve one-stop access to multiple blockchain network assets and applications.
This cooperation aims to combine SyncOrbit’s cross-chain account abstraction technology with MetaMask’s user base and user experience to jointly lower the threshold for users to enter and use the multi-chain Web3 world. In the future, further exploration will be made in account security, Gas optimization and other aspects.

data.title: BNB CHAIN ​​TV series RWA platform SYN VISION will be launched on Yunying Miner Ecosystem
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, the SYN VISION platform has delivered the smart contract to Certik for review. After the review is completed, the Yunying Ecological Miner Computing Power Whitelist Purchase Event will be officially opened on BNB CHAIN, and the platform token TWS and Yunying Miner Ecosystem will be launched later.
It is reported that SYN VISION is a community-driven, DAO-based film and television short drama RWA entertainment integrated platform, aiming to provide everyone with the opportunity to participate in film and television entertainment investment.
SYN VISION is invested by Malaysia's "W VENTURE CAPITAL", and the ASEAN operation team and the US technical team are responsible for operation and technical protection. PQMINER is the first Yunying Ecological Miner of SYN VISION, which is an AI smart destruction mining ecology based on BNB CHAIN. Yunying Ecological Miners are currently open for registration. Anyone who registers will get 5 TWS. After the smart contract review is completed, the whitelist purchase will be open, and the AI ​​smart mining function will also be open soon.

data.title: ArkStream Capital partner: OM is the ground-based OTC market, and the team is still promoting the "OTC market" business recently
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to HashKey Capital member Rui and ArkStream Capital Founding Partner Ye Su on X, the cryptocurrency project MANTRA (OM) is a "ground-pushing OTC plate" with an OTC scale of US$500 million. It adopts the mode of "new OTC tokens take over old OTC selling plates" and operates in a cycle until the last unlocked chips "OTC does not move" explode.
According to the revelation, in 2023, when the OM token FDV fell to US$20 million and was almost abandoned, a Middle Eastern capital intervened in the acquisition under the matchmaking of the middleman, retaining only the CEO position. The Middle Eastern capital owns a large number of physical assets such as luxury homes and resorts, and then packaged OM into a physical asset tokenization (RWAfi) project.
Ye Su said that under a high degree of control, OM set Binance's highest increase in 2024, achieving a 200-fold increase, and the team is still promoting the "OTC plate" business recently.

data.title: Odin.fun developer Bob Bodily’s account was hacked, clearing all his assets on the platform
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: On-chain data shows that the account of Odin.fun developer Bob Bodily was stolen, and all his assets on the platform were cleared. Affected by this, ODINDOG fell for a short time.

data.title: Solana Supercharges Its Ecosystem with Privacy Tech and Global Builder Events
data.description:
The Solana ecosystem surged with innovation last week, including programmable stablecoins, privacy tools, and major event announcements.

The post Solana Supercharges Its Ecosystem with Privacy Tech and Global Builder Events first appeared on Ecoinimist.

source: https://ecoinimist.com/2025/04/14/solana-supercharges-it-ecosystem/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=solana-supercharges-it-ecosystem

data.title: Citigroup downgrades U.S. stocks to neutral
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Citigroup equity strategists downgraded the U.S. stock market, saying that recent events such as DeepSeek, Europe's fiscal stance and trade tensions have reinforced their view on diversified investments outside the U.S. market. "From a GDP and EPS perspective, the drivers of 'exceptionalism' are fading," said Beata Manthey and other strategists at the bank, who downgraded the U.S. stock market from overweight to neutral. Citigroup said the U.S. market is still relatively expensive, and the downward trend in EPS expectations is intensifying. (Jinshi)

data.title: Crypto's TPS Obsession: A Misguided Race Ignoring Real User Needs
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the cryptocurrency industry's focus on achieving high transactions per second (TPS) is criticized as a misleading pursuit that overlooks genuine user needs. Projects often exaggerate lab data to attract funding and attention, sacrificing decentralization, security, and practicality to solve problems that few care about. The article calls for a focus on meaningful blockchain applications built to scale according to actual use cases, rather than chasing impressive but hollow numbers.

New Layer 1 or Layer 2 projects frequently claim to handle tens of thousands of transactions per second, but these figures are often unrealistic. The competition for scalability has become an embarrassing contest in the crypto world, with each new protocol claiming higher TPS than the last, regardless of whether these speeds are achievable outside of controlled environments like AWS testnets or relevant to real-world applications.

Visa, a global payment giant, processes about 1,700 transactions per second on average, with a theoretical maximum of 24,000 TPS, a capacity it has never needed in decades of operation. In contrast, most blockchain projects struggle to attract even 100 daily active users. If a project's Discord emojis outnumber its on-chain transactions, it may be addressing an imaginary problem.

The obsession with theoretical throughput has hidden costs. It often leads to disguised centralization, sacrificing decentralization for marketing numbers. Security is compromised as shortcuts are taken in the rush to scale, creating vulnerabilities. Talented engineers are diverted from building what users truly need to optimizing synthetic benchmarks. Moreover, the lab numbers touted in marketing often crumble under real-world conditions.

This fixation on extreme scalability is driven by the need to justify massive funding and stand out in a crowded market of over 5,000 blockchains. User needs are an afterthought, with the real goal being to convince retail investors of a project's ultimate solution status, while venture capitalists amplify the TPS narrative.

For those genuinely building in this space, the article advises focusing on what only blockchain can achieve, designing economic models that don't rely on constant new user acquisition, creating user-friendly interfaces, and scaling according to actual use cases rather than for impressive presentations.

When a project boasts about handling hundreds of thousands of TPS, it's crucial to ask what these transactions are doing, who is generating them, and for what purpose. True innovation lies not in theoretical performance but in building what people genuinely need, scaled appropriately to demand. Anything else is merely expensive performance art disguised as technology.
